ERC FUNDED PROJECTS | ERC: European Research Council offer simple closed-form descriptions for nonlinear phenomena that are difficult to work with in the conventional time- or frequency-domain, similar to how the conventional Fourier transform simplifies the analysis of linear systems. Today, almost fifty years after the discovery of the first nonlinear Fourier transform by Gardner et al., nonlinear Fourier transforms have been studied intensively in mathematics and physics. However, despite many potential applications, they have not yet found widespread use in engineering. The lack of efficient numerical algorithms for the computation of nonlinear Fourier transforms similar to the celebrated fast Fourier transform is the major roadblock. I have  Answer:

baseline.final_answer: The Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) is an efficient algorithm for computing the Fourier transform, reducing the time complexity from O(N^2) to O(N log N). It is widely used in various applications and is significantly faster than classical methods. Quantum computers can perform Fourier transforms even faster using the Quantum Fourier Transform (QFT), with a complexity of O((log N)^2) operations.

evaluation.feedback: The answer contains factual claims about the FFT's time complexity and quantum computing applications, but lacks any citations to support these claims. This violates the grounding policy.
